target: part3-NPxy-EXP436 c1 holes 1-3: source unity, WIN-007 ref, WIN-022 monitor claim (hole 4 acknowledged, no edit needed)

patches.find_pattern: from the same source: NOAA NGDC's North Magnetic Pole position record series (NP.xy), a model-reconstructed product blending gufm1 for the pre-1900 era with the IGRF series for the modern era (IGRF-14 is the current generation). This dataset is indexed and publicly available
patches.replace_pattern: from the same family of NOAA pole-position products — the NP.xy historical series (NOAA NGDC; a model-reconstructed product blending gufm1 for the pre-1900 era with the IGRF series for the modern era; IGRF-14 is the current generation) for WIN-006/007/022, and the closely-related World Magnetic Model (WMM) for WIN-036. Both products are indexed and publicly available
patches.description: ISS-2294 hole 1: Opening sentence claimed all four WINs draw from 'the same source: NP.xy'. WIN-036 uses WMM2025, which is a separate US/UK product built on IGRF-14 — related to NP.xy but distinct. New phrasing acknowledges both products by name and maps WIN-036 to WMM explicitly. 'Both products are indexed' replaces 'This dataset is indexed' to agree with the plural subject.

patches.find_pattern: hardcodes the post-1990 acceleration target on the same commit as the live-fetch line
patches.replace_pattern: hardcodes the target number on the same commit as the live-fetch line
patches.description: ISS-2294 hole 2: WIN-007 compressed reference. 'Post-1990 acceleration target' implies a numerical acceleration value is hardcoded; what Domain #11 actually hardcodes is a longitude threshold (130°E, trivially passing) and exponential-decay formula parameters. 'Target number' is the generic fallback the curmudgeon recommends — avoids the specific-but-misleading 'acceleration target' framing without losing the hardcoded-monitor finding.

patches.find_pattern: and unlike WIN-006/WIN-007 no monitor.py entry exists for this value at all — the dome author has not included it in the monitoring suite — so even the weaker commit-timestamp comparison
patches.replace_pattern: and unlike WIN-006/WIN-007 there is no dedicated monitor.py entry for WIN-022's phase-transition claim — Domain #11 is shared with WIN-007 (longitude threshold and exponential-decay formula) and does not specifically test the phase-transition assertion — so even the weaker commit-timestamp comparison
patches.description: ISS-2294 hole 3: 'No monitor.py entry exists for this value at all' contradicted WIN-022's own detail_evidence ('Domain #11 in monitor.py uses a hardcoded exponential decay formula...'). The fix clarifies that Domain #11 exists but is shared with WIN-007 and does not test WIN-022's specific phase-transition claim. This resolves the surface contradiction without altering the monitoring=none tag (which is correct: no DEDICATED entry).

hole4_note: Hole 4 (net char delta): EXP-436 claimed +540 chars net across the four WIN fields, but Part 3.1b grew by +2121 chars. The curmudgeon's recommended action for hole 4 was 'no edit; note it in the decider report.' Acknowledged here: the TF-IDF argument in EXP-436's anticipated_objections correctly defends the size increase, and the decider report for this run will carry the acknowledgment.
tldr_verdict: Three of four holes patched: (1) NPxy opening softened to acknowledge WMM vs NP.xy distinction; (2) WIN-007 'acceleration target' replaced with generic 'target number'; (3) WIN-022 'no entry' clarified to 'no dedicated entry — Domain #11 shared with WIN-007'. Hole 4 (char delta) acknowledged with no edit per curmudgeon recommendation.
